Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used before a singular noun when it is not specific. It means one.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: หนึ่ง, อันหนึ่ง
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have a book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Having the power or skill to do something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am able to swim.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: On the subject of; concerning. Or, approximately.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เกี่ยวกับ, ประมาณ
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is a book about animals. It's about 3 o'clock.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In or to a higher position than something else.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The plane is flying above the clouds.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: From one side to the other of something.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ข้าม, ทั่ว
Level 1: Beginner.example: He walked across the street.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To do something; to behave in a particular way.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: กระทำ, แสดง
Level 1: Beginner.example: She will act in a play.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Doing things, moving around a lot, and not just sitting or lying down.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: กระตือรือร้น, คล่องแคล่ว
Level 1: Beginner.example: He is an active child who likes to play outside.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Something that people do for enjoyment or as a job.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Playing games is a fun activity.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To join something to something else; to put two numbers together to find the total.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เพิ่ม, บวก
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please add sugar to your tea. 2 plus 2 add up to 4.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The number of the house, name of the street, etc. where a person lives or works.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is your home address?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A person who is fully grown or developed.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Children grow up to be adults.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Feeling fear or worry.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am afraid of the dark.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At a time later than a particular event or time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We will eat after the movie.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: One more time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you say that again, please?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The length of time that someone has lived or that something has existed.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is your age?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In the past.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I saw him two days ago.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To have the same opinion as someone else.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I agree with you.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The gas that we breathe.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We need clean air to live.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The whole amount or quantity of something; every one.
Level 1: Beginner.example: All the students are here.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To let someone do something; to permit.
Level 1: Beginner.example: My parents allow me to go out with friends.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In addition; too. It is used to add another piece of information.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ด้วย, เช่นกัน
Level 1: Beginner.example: I like apples, and I also like bananas.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At all times; on all occasions. Something that happens very often.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เสมอ, ตลอดเวลา
Level 1: Beginner.example: She always wakes up early.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to connect words, clauses, or sentences that are to be taken together.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have a cat and a dog.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Feeling or showing strong annoyance, displeasure, or hostility.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He was angry because he lost his toy.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A living thing that is not a plant, such as a dog, cat, or bird.
Level 1: Beginner.example: A dog is a friendly animal.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: One more; an additional one.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: อีกอันหนึ่ง, อีกคนหนึ่ง
Level 1: Beginner.example: Would you like another cup of tea?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A spoken or written response to a question.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please answer the question.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to refer to one or some of a thing or number of things, no matter how much or how many.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Do you have any questions?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A particular part of a place, city, or country.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: พื้นที่, บริเวณ
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is a safe area.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The part of the human body between the shoulder and the wrist.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He broke his arm when he fell.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In a circle or other circular shape.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The children played around the tree.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Reach a place at the end of a journey or a stage in a journey.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The train will arrive soon.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Say something in order to obtain an answer or information.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can I ask you a question?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to indicate location or a specific point in time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She is at home.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To or at a distance from a particular place, person, or thing.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please go away.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A very young child, especially one newly born.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ทารก, เด็กอ่อน
Level 1: Beginner.example: The baby is sleeping.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The part of the human body opposite the front, extending from the neck to the hips.
Level 1: Beginner.example: My back hurts.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Of poor quality or a low standard.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ไม่ดี, แย่
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is a bad idea.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A round object used in games or sports.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He kicked the ball.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to indicate existence, identity, or a state of being.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เป็น, อยู่, คือ
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am happy.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to explain why something happens or is true.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am happy because it is sunny.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To start to be something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The caterpillar will become a butterfly.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Earlier than a particular time or event.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Wash your hands before you eat.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To start doing something or to start happening.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The movie will begin in five minutes.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At the back of someone or something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The cat is hiding behind the sofa.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To think that something is true or real.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I believe you can do it.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The most good or excellent.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is the best cake I have ever eaten.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: More good than someone or something else.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This book is better than the last one.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In the space separating two things or people.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The park is between the school and the library.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Large in size.
Level 1: Beginner.example: That is a big dog.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The color of coal or the night sky.
Level 1: Beginner.example: My car is black.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The color of the sky on a clear day.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The sky is blue today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A set of written or printed pages bound together.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am reading a good book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Brought into life by birth.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I was born in 2005.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The two people or things already mentioned.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Both my parents are teachers.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A container with flat sides and a lid.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Put the toys in the box.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A male child or young man.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เด็กผู้ชาย
Level 1: Beginner.example: The boy is playing with a ball.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To separate into pieces or to make something separate into pieces.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: แตก, หยุดพัก
Level 1: Beginner.example: Be careful not to break the glass.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To take or carry someone or something to a place.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you bring me a glass of water?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A boy or man who has the same parents as you.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: พี่ชาย, น้องชาย
Level 1: Beginner.example: My brother is older than me.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make something by putting parts together. You can build a house or a toy.
Level 1: Beginner.example: They will build a new school.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A large vehicle that carries many people. You can take a bus to go to another place.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: รถประจำทาง
Level 1: Beginner.example: I take the bus to school every day.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to introduce a word or phrase that contrasts with what has just been said. It shows a difference.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want to go, but I am tired.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To get something by paying money for it. You buy food at the shop.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I need to buy some milk.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Near or next to something. It can also mean how something is done.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: โดย, ข้างๆ
Level 1: Beginner.example: The book is by the window.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To speak to someone on the phone. You can also call someone's name.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: โทร, เรียก
Level 1: Beginner.example: I will call you later.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To be able to do something. It shows ability.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I can speak English.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A vehicle with four wheels that is used for travelling. You drive a car.
Level 1: Beginner.example: My father has a new car.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To feel and show that you are interested in someone or something. To look after someone.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ดูแล, ใส่ใจ
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please care for your pet.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To hold something and take it from one place to another. To move something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you carry this bag for me?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A small animal with soft fur, a tail, and sharp claws. Many people have cats as pets.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The cat is sleeping on the sofa.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make or become different. To give one thing and get another.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The weather can change quickly.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A young boy or girl. A person who is not yet an adult.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The child is playing in the park.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A large town. A place with many buildings and people.
Level 1: Beginner.example: London is a very big city.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A group of students who are taught together. A lesson.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have an English class today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make something free from dirt or marks. Not dirty.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: สะอาด, ทำความสะอาด
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please clean your room.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Easy to see or understand. Not cloudy or dirty.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ชัดเจน, ใส
Level 1: Beginner.example: The sky is clear today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To shut something. To be near to something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please close the door.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Things that people wear, such as shirts, trousers, and dresses. What you wear.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I need to buy new clothes.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To move towards the place where someone is. To arrive.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you come here, please?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The amount of money you have to pay for something. It is the price of an item.
Level 1: Beginner.example: How much does this book cost?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A nation with its own government, occupying a particular territory. It is a place like France or Japan.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want to visit another country.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make something shorter or smaller with a sharp tool. You can cut paper or hair.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please cut the bread into slices.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Without light. It is the opposite of bright.
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is too dark to see outside.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The period of time from sunrise to sunset. It is the opposite of night.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Today is a beautiful day.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: No longer alive. A plant or animal that is dead does not move or breathe.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The flowers in the vase are dead.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A flat, movable barrier used to close an opening in a wall or building. You open and close it to go in or out.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please close the door when you leave.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Towards a lower place or position. It is the opposite of up.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The ball rolled down the hill.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make a picture or design using a pen or pencil. You can draw a house or a person.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you draw a cat?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A series of thoughts, images, and sensations occurring in a person's mind during sleep. You can also dream about something you want to happen.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I had a strange dream last night.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To take liquid into your mouth and swallow it. You drink water or juice.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want to drink some water.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To operate and control the direction and speed of a motor vehicle. You drive a car or a bus.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He can drive a car.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To let something fall. You might drop your keys.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Be careful not to drop the glass.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Throughout the course or duration of a period of time. It means 'in the time of'.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We talked during dinner.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Every one of two or more people or things, regarded separately. It refers to every single item.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Each student has a book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Happening or done before the usual or expected time. It is the opposite of late.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I woke up early this morning.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To put food into your mouth and chew and swallow it. You eat breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Let's eat lunch together.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The final part of something. It is the opposite of the beginning.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The movie will end soon.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To take pleasure in something. You enjoy a party or a good book.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I enjoy listening to music.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To come or go into a place. You enter a building or a room.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please enter the classroom quietly.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to refer to all the separate members of a group or all the times of a series.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Every student has a book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A thing that is representative of a group of things. A person or thing that is typical of a kind.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is an example of a good sentence.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The organ of sight. It is used for seeing.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She has blue eyes.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The front part of a person's head from the forehead to the chin. It has eyes, a nose, and a mouth.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He has a happy face.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To move downwards to the ground or a lower level. To drop.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The leaves fall from the trees in autumn.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A group of people who are related to each other, such as parents and children.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I love my family very much.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At, to, or by a great distance.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The shop is far from here.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To experience an emotion or sensation. To touch something to find out what it is like.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I feel happy today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To discover something or someone by searching. To get something after a search.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I can't find my keys.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To complete something. To come to the end of an activity.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Let's finish our homework now.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The burning of something, producing heat and light. Flames.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Be careful not to touch the fire.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Coming before all others in order, time, or importance.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: แรก, ที่หนึ่ง
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is my first time here.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Something that people or animals eat. It gives energy.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is your favorite food?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to indicate the purpose or recipient of an action or thing. Used to indicate a duration of time.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: สำหรับ, เพื่อ
Level 1: Beginner.example: This gift is for you.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To fail to remember something. To not think about something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I often forget his name.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not costing money. Able to do what you want.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ฟรี, อิสระ
Level 1: Beginner.example: This book is free.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A person whom one knows and with whom one has a bond of mutual affection.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She is my best friend.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to indicate the starting point of a journey or movement. Used to indicate the source of something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am from Thailand.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The part of an object that is furthest forward or facing forward. The side that is seen first.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The car is in the front yard.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Containing or holding as much or as many as possible. Having eaten enough food.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The glass is full of water.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An activity that you do for fun or to win. It often has rules.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Let's play a game.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To receive something or to become something. It can also mean to arrive somewhere.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ได้รับ, ไปถึง
Level 1: Beginner.example: I will get a new book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A young female person.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เด็กผู้หญิง
Level 1: Beginner.example: The girl is reading a book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To hand something to someone. To let someone have something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you give me that pen?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To move from one place to another. To travel.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want to go to the park.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Of a high standard or quality. Pleasant or enjoyable.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is a good idea.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Very good or enjoyable. Very large in size or amount.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ยอดเยี่ยม, ใหญ่
Level 1: Beginner.example: We had a great time.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The color of grass and leaves. A color between blue and yellow.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The tree has green leaves.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A number of people or things that are together or that are similar.
Level 1: Beginner.example: A group of friends went to the cinema.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To get bigger or taller. To develop.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Children grow very fast.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The part of the body at the end of the arm. It has fingers and a thumb.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please lend me a hand.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To take place. To occur.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What did happen last night?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Feeling or showing pleasure or contentment. Glad.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She is happy to see you.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Solid and firm. Difficult to do or understand.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is a hard question.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To own or possess something. To experience something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have a dog.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to refer to a man or boy that has already been mentioned.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He is my brother.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The top part of the body that contains the brain, eyes, and mouth.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ศีรษะ, หัว
Level 1: Beginner.example: My head hurts.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To sense sound with your ears.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you hear the music?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make it easier for someone to do something. To assist.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you help me with this bag?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In, at, or to this place or position.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please come here.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Something that is high is a long way up from the ground. It can also mean a large amount.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The mountain is very high.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: This word shows that something belongs to a boy or a man.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is his book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Home is the place where you live, like a house or an apartment.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am going home now.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To hope means to want something to happen and think that it might.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I hope to see you soon.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Hot means having a high temperature. It can also mean spicy.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The soup is very hot.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A house is a building where people live.
Level 1: Beginner.example: They live in a big house.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: How is used to ask about the way something is done or the condition of something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: How are you today?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An idea is a thought or a suggestion about what to do.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have a good idea.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: If is used to talk about a possible situation or to introduce a condition.
Level 1: Beginner.example: If it rains, we will stay inside.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Something that is important is very valuable or significant.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is an important message.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In is used to show that something is inside something else or in a place.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The cat is in the box.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: It is used to refer to a thing, an animal, or a situation that has already been mentioned.
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is a beautiful day.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To keep means to continue to have something or to not let something change.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please keep the door open.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To know means to have information about something or someone.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I know the answer.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Large means big in size or amount.
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is a large car.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Last means happening or coming after all other things. It can also mean the most recent.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is the last page.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Late means happening or arriving after the usual or expected time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He arrived late for the meeting.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To learn means to gain knowledge or skill by studying or practicing.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want to learn English.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To leave means to go away from a place.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We will leave tomorrow.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To let means to allow someone to do something or to allow something to happen.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Let me help you.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The state of being alive. It is the time between birth and death.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He has a happy life.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The brightness that comes from the sun or a lamp. It helps us to see.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Turn on the light, please.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To enjoy something or think that it is good. You can also use it to say something is similar.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ชอบ, เหมือน
Level 1: Beginner.example: I like pizza.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To pay attention to sounds with your ears.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please listen to the music.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To be alive. It can also mean to stay in a place.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: อาศัยอยู่, มีชีวิต
Level 1: Beginner.example: I live in a big city.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Measuring a great distance from end to end. It can also mean for a great amount of time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The road is very long.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To use your eyes to see something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Look at the beautiful sky.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To not have something anymore. It can also mean to not win a game.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: สูญเสีย, แพ้
Level 1: Beginner.example: Don't lose your keys.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To like someone or something very much. It is a strong feeling of liking.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I love my family.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not high. It can describe height, sound, or temperature.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The table is very low.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To create or build something. It can also mean to cause something to happen.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She can make a cake.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An adult male human being.
Level 1: Beginner.example: That is a tall man.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A large number of people or things.
Level 1: Beginner.example: There are many books on the shelf.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to show that something is possible but not certain.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: บางที, อาจจะ
Level 1: Beginner.example: Maybe it will rain tomorrow.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To have a particular meaning. It can also mean to intend to do something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What does this word mean?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To come to a place where someone is. It can also mean to be introduced to someone for the first time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I will meet you at the park.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Coins and paper that are used as a way of buying things.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I need some money to buy food.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A greater amount or number of something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want more ice cream.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The largest amount or number. It can also mean the majority of something.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ส่วนใหญ่, ที่สุด
Level 1: Beginner.example: Most people like to sleep.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To change your position or place. It can also mean to make something change its position.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เคลื่อนไหว, ย้าย
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you move the chair?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: What you call a person, place, or thing. It is how you identify someone.
Level 1: Beginner.example: My name is John.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To want or require something very much. You must have it.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I need a glass of water.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At no time in the past or future. It means not ever.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have never seen a lion.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Something that has not existed before, or has not been seen, heard, or experienced before. It is the opposite of old.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have a new car.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Coming immediately after the present time or in order. It is the one after this.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is the next word?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The period of darkness between sunset and sunrise. It is when you sleep.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Good night!

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to give a negative response. It means the opposite of yes.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Do you want coffee? No, thank you.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At the present time. It is happening at this moment.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am busy now.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A mathematical value that represents a count or quantity. It is a digit or a series of digits.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is your phone number?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not on or working. It can also mean away from a place.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please turn the light off.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Many times. It happens frequently.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I often go to the park.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Having lived for a long time. It is the opposite of new.
Level 1: Beginner.example: My grandfather is old.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In contact with and supported by a surface. It can also mean working or active.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The book is on the table.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: And no one or nothing else. Just one.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have only one apple.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not closed or blocked up. You can go through it.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The door is open.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to connect two or more alternatives. It gives a choice.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Do you want tea or coffee?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Different from one already mentioned or known. It is another one.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have another pen; this one is red, and the other is blue.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Belonging to us. It is something that we have.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is our house.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Away from a place or in a direction away from a place. Not inside.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please go out.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Above or higher than something else. It can also mean finished.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The plane flew over the city.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To have something that belongs to you. It is yours.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เป็นของตัวเอง
Level 1: Beginner.example: I have my own car.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A piece or section of something. It is not the whole thing.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is a part of the book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To give money for something you buy or for a service.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I need to pay for this coffee.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: More than one person. It means men, women, and children.
Level 1: Beginner.example: There are many people in the park.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: One human being. A man, woman, or child.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She is a kind person.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An idea about what you are going to do. A way to do something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We have a plan for the weekend.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To do an activity for fun. To play a game or with toys.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Children like to play in the garden.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to be polite when you ask for something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you help me, please?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A small dot. Or, to show something with your finger.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He made a point on the map.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Something that can happen or be done. It is not impossible.
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is possible to finish this today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To move something to a particular place. To place it there.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please put the book on the table.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To look at words and understand them. To read a book or a sign.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I like to read stories.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Prepared to do something. You are able to start.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Are you ready to go?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A color. Like the color of blood or a stop sign.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The apple is red.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Correct or true. Or, the opposite of left.
Level 1: Beginner.example: That is the right answer.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A part of a house or building that has walls and a door. Like a bedroom or living room.
Level 1: Beginner.example: My bedroom is small.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To move fast on your feet. Faster than walking.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The dog can run very fast.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not different. Exactly alike.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We have the same toy.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To speak words. To tell someone something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What did you say?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A place where children go to learn. Teachers teach students here.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I go to school every day.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To notice something with your eyes. You can see with your eyes.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I can see the cat.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To appear to be something or to be a certain way. It looks like it.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She seems happy.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to refer to a woman or girl. It is a word for a girl or woman.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เธอ (ผู้หญิง)
Level 1: Beginner.example: She is my sister.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not long in length or height. Not tall.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He is a short man.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To let someone see something. To make something visible.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: แสดง, ให้ดู
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you show me your book?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The part of something that is not the front, back, top, or bottom. The left or right part.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The house has a garden on the side.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not big in size. Little.
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is a small car.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to say that something is true to a great degree. Very.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ดังนั้น, มาก
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is so cold today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An amount or number of something that is not known or not stated. A little bit.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: บางส่วน, จำนวนหนึ่ง
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want some water.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At some times, but not always. Now and then.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Sometimes I eat ice cream.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A boy or man in relation to his parents. A father's or mother's boy.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He is my son.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In a short time from now. Not in a long time.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เร็วๆ นี้
Level 1: Beginner.example: I will see you soon.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To begin doing something. To make something begin.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Let's start the game.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To remain in a particular place or position. To not leave.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I will stay at home.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Continuing to happen or exist. Not stopped.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He is still sleeping.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To finish doing something. To not continue.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please stop talking.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An account of events, real or imaginary. A tale.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I like to read a story.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To try to learn about a subject by reading, going to school, etc. To learn.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เรียน, ศึกษา
Level 1: Beginner.example: I study English.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Of this kind. Like this.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เช่นนั้น, อย่างนั้น
Level 1: Beginner.example: It was such a good movie.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To get something from someone or something. To carry or bring.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เอา, รับ, พาไป
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can I take your pen?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To speak in order to give information or express ideas. To have a conversation with someone.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I want to talk to you about something.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To say something to someone, especially to give them information. To make something known.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you tell me the time?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to compare two people or things. It shows a difference between two things.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This book is better than that one.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to identify a specific person or thing that is a little far away. Used to refer to something already mentioned.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Look at that bird!

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used before a noun to refer to a specific person or thing that is known to the listener or reader.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The dog is sleeping.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Belonging to or associated with the people or things previously mentioned. It shows possession for more than one person.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is their house.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used as the object of a verb or preposition to refer to two or more people or things previously mentioned.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I saw them at the park.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: At that time. After that; next.
Level 1: Beginner.example: First, we eat, then we go home.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: In, at, or to that place or position. Used to introduce a sentence or clause.
Level 1: Beginner.example: There is a cat on the roof.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to refer to two or more people or things previously mentioned. The plural subject pronoun.
Level 1: Beginner.example: They are happy.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An object or entity that is not specified or named. Something that is done or happens.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is that thing?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To have a particular opinion, belief, or idea about someone or something. To use your mind to consider something.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I think it will rain today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to identify a specific person or thing that is close by. Used to refer to something that is happening now.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is my book.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The ongoing sequence of events taking place. The moment when something happens.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What time is it?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Used to indicate movement towards a place or person. Used to indicate the person or thing that is affected by an action.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am going to the store.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: On this day; on the present day. The current day.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Today is a sunny day.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: With or near each other. In company or in conjunction.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Let's eat together.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: More than enough; excessively. Also; in addition.
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is too hot today.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The highest part or point of something. The best or most important.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The cat is on top of the house.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A place where people live and work, larger than a village but smaller than a city.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I live in a small town.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To give someone something, like a prize, because they have done something good or won something. It can also be the prize itself.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She won an award for her excellent work.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The bottom part of something, or the main place where a group of people live or work. It can also be a starting point.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ฐาน, ที่ตั้ง
Level 1: Beginner.example: The house has a strong base made of stone.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Simple and easy to understand or do. It is the most important or fundamental part of something.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: พื้นฐาน, ง่ายๆ
Level 1: Beginner.example: He learned the basic rules of the game.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To be in the right place, or to be a part of a group. It means to feel comfortable and accepted.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เป็นของ, อยู่ในกลุ่ม
Level 1: Beginner.example: This book belongs on the shelf.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Next to someone or something. It means close to them.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The cat sat beside the dog.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Further away than something, or past a certain point. It can also mean more than what is expected.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ไกลกว่า, เกินกว่า
Level 1: Beginner.example: The park is just beyond that hill.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A small piece of something, or a short period of time. It means a little bit.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เล็กน้อย, ชิ้นเล็กๆ
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can I have a bit of your cake?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To move air, or to make a sound by forcing air out of your mouth. It can also mean to hit something hard.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The wind began to blow strongly.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To take something from someone and promise to give it back later. You use it for a period of time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can I borrow your pen for a moment?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A part of a tree that grows out from the main trunk. It can also be a part of a larger organization.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: กิ่งไม้, สาขา
Level 1: Beginner.example: A bird was sitting on a high branch.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To be on fire, or to damage something with fire or heat. It can also mean to feel very hot.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Be careful not to burn yourself on the stove.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To stop and hold something that is moving. It can also mean to get an illness.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He tried to catch the ball.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make something happen. It is the reason why something occurs.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: สาเหตุ, ทำให้เกิด
Level 1: Beginner.example: Loud noise can cause damage to your hearing.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Knowing something is true, or sure to happen. You are confident about it.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: แน่นอน, บางอย่าง
Level 1: Beginner.example: I am certain that he will come.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An opportunity to do something, or the possibility of something happening. It is about luck or possibility.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: โอกาส, ความเป็นไปได้
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is your chance to win the prize.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To look at something to see if it is correct or if it is okay. It can also be a written order for money.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ตรวจสอบ, เช็ค
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please check your answers before you hand in the test.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To decide what you want from a selection of things. You make a choice.
Level 1: Beginner.example: You can choose any color you like.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A round shape with no corners. It is a line that is the same distance from the center everywhere.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Draw a circle on the paper.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To bring together things that are similar, or to get things from different people. It can also mean to pick something up.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: สะสม, เก็บ
Level 1: Beginner.example: She likes to collect stamps from different countries.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A place where students go after high school to study for a degree. It is a higher education institution.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: วิทยาลัย, มหาวิทยาลัย
Level 1: Beginner.example: He is studying engineering at college.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The light that your eyes see from something. It can be red, blue, green, etc.
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is your favourite colour?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To look at two or more things to see how they are the same or different.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เปรียบเทียบ
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you compare these two books?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To finish doing something. To make something whole.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ทำให้เสร็จ, สมบูรณ์
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please complete the form before you leave.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To have something inside. To hold something.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: บรรจุ, ประกอบด้วย
Level 1: Beginner.example: This box can contain many books.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To keep doing something without stopping. To go on.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ดำเนินต่อไป
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please continue reading the story.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To have the power to make someone or something do what you want. To manage.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The driver must control the car carefully.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make something right. To fix a mistake.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ถูกต้อง, แก้ไข
Level 1: Beginner.example: Is this the correct answer?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make something new. To invent.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: สร้างสรรค์
Level 1: Beginner.example: The artist will create a beautiful painting.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Likely to cause harm or injury. Not safe.
Level 1: Beginner.example: It is dangerous to play near the road.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To give attention to something. To do business with someone.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: จัดการ, ข้อตกลง
Level 1: Beginner.example: We need to deal with this problem quickly.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make a choice about something. To make up your mind.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I need to decide what to wear.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Going far down from the top or surface. Not shallow.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The ocean is very deep.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To say what something or someone is like. To tell about.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you describe the person you saw?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To break something so that it cannot be used or fixed. To ruin.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The storm can destroy houses.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A small part of something. A specific piece of information.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: รายละเอียด
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please give me more detail about the plan.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To grow or change and become more advanced. To make something grow.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The company will develop new products.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To stop living. To pass away.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Plants can die if they don't get water.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The way in which two or more things are not the same.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ความแตกต่าง
Level 1: Beginner.example: What is the difference between these two colours?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Not easy to do or understand. Hard.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This math problem is very difficult.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The way that someone or something moves or faces. A path.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Which direction should we go?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make a sound with your voice when you are happy or amused. It is a way of showing you find something funny.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She started to laugh when she heard the joke.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To guide or show someone the way to a place. It can also mean to be in charge or in front.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: นำ, เป็นผู้นำ
Level 1: Beginner.example: The teacher will lead the students on a field trip.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A position or rank on a scale. It can also mean flat and straight.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ระดับ, แบนราบ
Level 1: Beginner.example: The water in the pool is at a good level.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The greatest amount or number that is allowed. It is a point beyond which something cannot go.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ขีดจำกัด, จำกัด
Level 1: Beginner.example: There is a speed limit of 50 kilometers per hour on this road.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Belonging to or happening in a particular area or neighborhood. It is not far away.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ท้องถิ่น, ในพื้นที่
Level 1: Beginner.example: We bought some fresh fruit from a local market.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The most important or largest part of something. It is the primary thing.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: หลัก, สำคัญ
Level 1: Beginner.example: The main reason for the delay was the bad weather.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To be in charge of or to succeed in doing something, especially something difficult. It means to control or organize.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: จัดการ, บริหาร
Level 1: Beginner.example: She managed to finish the project on time.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A game or contest, especially in sports. It can also mean to be the same or similar.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: การแข่งขัน, คู่ที่เข้ากัน
Level 1: Beginner.example: Did you watch the football match last night?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A subject or situation that is being discussed or considered. It can also mean to be important.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เรื่อง, สิ่งสำคัญ
Level 1: Beginner.example: It doesn't matter if you are a little late.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To find the size, amount, or degree of something. It is to calculate or estimate.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: วัด, มาตรการ
Level 1: Beginner.example: We need to measure the length of the room.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The ability to remember things. It is also the things that you remember.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ความจำ, ความทรงจำ
Level 1: Beginner.example: She has a good memory for faces.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A period of time equal to sixty seconds. It is a short amount of time.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I'll be there in a few minutes.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To fail to hit, catch, or reach something. It can also mean to feel sad because someone or something is not there.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: พลาด, คิดถึง
Level 1: Beginner.example: He will miss his train if he doesn't hurry.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To combine or put together different things. It is to blend.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ผสม, คลุกเคล้า
Level 1: Beginner.example: Let's mix the flour and sugar together.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Belonging to the present time or recent times. It is not old-fashioned.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ทันสมัย, สมัยใหม่
Level 1: Beginner.example: They live in a modern apartment building.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A large natural rise in the surface of the earth. It is much higher than a hill.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We climbed the mountain to see the view.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The opening in the face of a person or animal used for eating, speaking, and breathing. It is also the opening of a river into the sea.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He opened his mouth to speak.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Existing in nature and not made or caused by people. It is normal or expected.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ธรรมชาติ, โดยธรรมชาติ
Level 1: Beginner.example: The park has beautiful natural scenery.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To present something to someone for them to accept or refuse. It is to suggest something.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: เสนอ, ข้อเสนอ
Level 1: Beginner.example: They offered me a job in their company.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A request for something to be made, supplied, or served. It can also mean to tell someone to do something.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: คำสั่ง, ลำดับ, สั่งซื้อ
Level 1: Beginner.example: I would like to order a pizza, please.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Easy to understand or do. Not complicated.
Level 1: Beginner.example: This is a simple recipe that anyone can follow.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: An ability to do something well. It is something you learn to do.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Learning to ride a bike is a useful skill.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To rest your body and mind by closing your eyes and not moving. You do this at night.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I need to get eight hours of sleep tonight.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To make a happy expression on your face by turning up the corners of your mouth.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She gave me a big smile when she saw me.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Easy to press or bend. Not hard.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The kitten's fur was very soft.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To find the answer to a problem or question.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Can you help me solve this math problem?

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To put things into different groups or piles based on what they are like.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please sort the clean clothes into piles.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Something that you can hear. It is made by vibrations.
Level 1: Beginner.example: I heard a strange sound outside my window.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To say words. To talk to someone.
Level 1: Beginner.example: She can speak three languages.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: How fast something moves or happens.
Level 1: Beginner.example: The car was going at a high speed.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To use time or money to do something.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ใช้ (เวลา/เงิน)
Level 1: Beginner.example: I like to spend my free time reading books.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To be in an upright position on your feet. Not sitting or lying down.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Please stand up when the teacher enters the room.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: To move by lifting your foot and putting it down again. A single movement of your foot.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Take one step forward and then stop.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Unusual or surprising. Not normal.
Level 1: Beginner.example: That was a strange dream I had last night.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A public road in a city or town, with houses and buildings on one or both sides.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We live on a quiet street.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Having a lot of power or strength. Not weak.
Level 1: Beginner.example: He is very strong and can lift heavy boxes.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: A topic or idea that is discussed or written about. A school class.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: หัวข้อ, วิชา
Level 1: Beginner.example: My favorite subject at school is art.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The achievement of something that you have tried to do. Doing well.
Level 1: Beginner.thai: ความสำเร็จ
Level 1: Beginner.example: Hard work often leads to success.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: Happening quickly and unexpectedly.
Level 1: Beginner.example: Suddenly, the lights went out.

Level 1: Beginner.definition: The warmest season of the year, between spring and autumn.
Level 1: Beginner.example: We like to go to the beach in the summer.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: Having or showing imagination and the ability to make new things or think of new ideas. It's about being original and inventive.
Level 2: Intermediate.thai: สร้างสรรค์
Level 2: Intermediate.example: She has a very creative approach to solving problems.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: An illegal act that is punishable by law. It is something that people do that is against the rules of society.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: The police are investigating a serious crime that happened last night.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: A time of great danger or difficulty, when a difficult decision must be made. It's a very serious situation.
Level 2: Intermediate.thai: วิกฤตการณ์
Level 2: Intermediate.example: The country is facing an economic crisis.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: The ideas, customs, and social behaviour of a particular people or society. It includes their art, music, and way of life.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: Learning about a new culture can be very interesting.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: Happening or existing now. It refers to the present time or the most recent period.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: What is the current news about the election?

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: A person who buys goods or services from a shop or business. They are the people who purchase things.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: The shop assistant was very helpful to the customer.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: Harm that is done to something or someone, making it weaker or less valuable. It can be physical or emotional.
Level 2: Intermediate.thai: ความเสียหาย
Level 2: Intermediate.example: The storm caused a lot of damage to the houses.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: A formal discussion on a particular matter in a public meeting or legislative assembly, in which opposing arguments are put forward. It's a discussion with different opinions.
Level 2: Intermediate.thai: การโต้วาที
Level 2: Intermediate.example: There was a lively debate about the new law.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: To explain the meaning of a word or concept. It's about stating what something is or what it means.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: Can you define the word 'ambiguous' for me?

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: A qualification awarded by a college or university to a student who has completed a course of study. It can also mean a level or amount.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: She has a degree in history from a famous university.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: To take goods, letters, etc. to the place or person they have been sent to. It can also mean to give a speech.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: The postman will deliver the package tomorrow.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: An insistent request, made as if by right. It's when people strongly ask for something.
Level 2: Intermediate.thai: ความต้องการ
Level 2: Intermediate.example: There is a high demand for tickets to the concert.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: To show clearly how something works or how to do something. It can also mean to show publicly that you are angry or unhappy.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: The teacher will demonstrate how to use the new software.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: To be determined or decided by something. It means that something will happen if another thing happens.
Level 2: Intermediate.thai: ขึ้นอยู่กับ
Level 2: Intermediate.example: Our plans for the weekend depend on the weather.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: A plan or drawing produced to show the look and function or workings of a building, garment, or other object before it is made. It's about creating a plan or pattern.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: He has a talent for graphic design.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: A strong feeling of wanting to have something or wishing for something to happen. It's a deep wish.
Level 2: Intermediate.thai: ความปรารถนา
Level 2: Intermediate.example: She has a strong desire to travel the world.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: Without being affected by; in spite of. It means that something happens even though there is a problem or difficulty.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: Despite the rain, they went for a walk.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: To discover or identify the presence or existence of something. It's about finding something that is hidden or difficult to see.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: The alarm system can detect intruders.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: To find out or establish exactly, typically as a result of research or calculation. It means to decide something firmly.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: Scientists are trying to determine the cause of the disease.

Level 2: Intermediate.definition: To be unlike or dissimilar. It means to be different from something else.
Level 2: Intermediate.example: Our opinions on this matter differ greatly.
